_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: The ability to generate or use different sets of rules for combining or grouping things in different ways.
reference_document_element_description: Ability to adapt risk management frameworks developed for traditional data and analytics to emerging AI/ML contexts, recognizing where established approaches apply directly, where they need modification, and where entirely new frameworks are required.
comments: Category flexibility — applying different frameworks to the same problem — is expressed in WIDAI through framework adaptation (RM-A-004), governance calibration (AG-A-001), and analytical method selection (AB-A-002).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: Moderate similarity (nist band: roughly as similar as dissimilar). The moderate STS score reflects a genuine but bounded intersection. The overlapping concepts are detectable but represent a subset of what each description covers independently.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: F1 of 0.8521 indicates lower token-level semantic alignment. Precision exceeds Recall, indicating that the FDE's semantic content is more tightly aligned with the KSA than the reverse — the KSA contains concepts or specificity beyond what the FDE covers. For an Intersects relationship, the P/R balance indicates which direction the overlap tilts — whether the FDE captures more of the KSA's content or vice versa.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: Neutral-dominant (99.93%) — the model finds the descriptions related but neither implying nor contradicting each other. Neutral is the expected NLI outcome for Intersects relationships — the descriptions share conceptual territory without one implying the other.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: The bi-encoder score (0.2084) is substantially lower than the STS cross-encoder (0.4507), a gap of 0.24. This divergence is diagnostic: the cross-encoder captures cross-text semantic interactions that the bi-encoder's independent encoding misses. The relationship between these descriptions depends on contextual meaning that only emerges when both texts are processed together.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Low lexical overlap (7.14% of tokens shared). Some shared vocabulary exists (likely common functional words or broadly-used domain terms) but the bulk of each description uses distinct terminology.
